question: [File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a crafted transactionIdOrHash path variable that resolves to a different transaction than the one originally executed, causing the opcode replay to present a falsified execution history for that transaction ID?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the lack of per-user throttling in throttleOpcodeRequest() to flood the endpoint with concurrent replay requests for the same transaction, causing non-deterministic opcode traces that misrepresent the canonical execution history?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged user submit a transactionIdOrHash that matches a hash collision in contractTransactionHashRepository, causing the endpoint to replay a different contract's transaction and return its opcode trace under the queried hash, reorganizing the visible execution history?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged user bypass the properties.isEnabled() feature flag check by exploiting a race condition during dynamic configuration reload, causing the endpoint to serve opcode traces when it should return 404, exposing historical transaction data that reorganizes the perceived execution record?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: validateAcceptEncodingHeader()] Can an unprivileged user craft an Accept-Encoding header value such as 'GZIP' or 'x-gzip' that passes the toLowerCase().contains('gzip') check but causes downstream decompression to fail or misinterpret the response, leading to corrupted opcode trace data being presented as the canonical transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged user submit a TransactionIdParameter where the validStartNs maps to multiple transactions in transactionRepository, causing transactionList.getFirst() to select the wrong parent transaction and replay a different transaction's opcodes under the queried ID, reorganizing transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fallback to BlockType.LATEST when recordFileService.findByTimestamp() returns empty, causing the EVM replay to execute the transaction against the current state rather than the historical block state, producing an opcode trace that misrepresents the original execution history?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged user supply a transaction hash that exists in contractTransactionHashRepository but whose associated consensusTimestamp has no matching record in contractResultRepository, causing an EntityNotFoundException that leaks internal timestamp values and reveals the structure of the transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged user craft a TransactionIdParameter with a payerAccountId and validStartNs that matches a scheduled or child transaction rather than the parent, causing buildCallServiceParameters() to replay the wrong transaction and present a reorganized execution trace?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the OpcodeContext gas calculation '(int) params.getGas() / 3' integer truncation to cause the replay to run with a different gas limit than the original transaction, producing an opcode trace with different execution paths (e.g., OOG at a different point) that misrepresents the original transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged user trigger the opcodeRateLimitBucket exhaustion by sending requests at the exact rate limit boundary, causing legitimate users' requests to be throttled while the attacker's last request before the limit returns a stale or incorrect opcode trace, effectively reorganizing what history is visible?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged user submit a TransactionHashParameter whose hash bytes match a contractTransactionHash entry but whose payerAccountId in that entry differs from the actual Ethereum transaction's payer, causing ethereumTransactionRepository.findByConsensusTimestampAndPayerAccountId() to return null and the replay to use wrong call data, reorganizing the visible execution trace?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the ContractCallContext.run() context isolation to cause context state from a concurrent request to bleed into another request's OpcodeContext, producing a mixed opcode trace that combines execution history from two different transactions?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged user supply a transactionIdOrHash that is valid as both a TransactionHashParameter and a TransactionIdParameter format, causing ambiguous parsing in TransactionIdOrHashParameter.valueOf() and potentially resolving to a different transaction than intended, reorganizing the returned execution history?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the stack=true default parameter to force the replay to include stack data that was not captured in the original execution, causing the opcode trace to contain fabricated stack frames that misrepresent the original transaction's execution history?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged user send concurrent requests for the same transactionIdOrHash with different combinations of stack/memory/storage flags, causing the ContractCallContext to be set with conflicting OpcodeContext configurations and returning inconsistent opcode traces for the same transaction, reorganizing the perceived execution history?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the absence of authentication on the /api/v1/contracts/results/{transactionIdOrHash}/opcodes endpoint to enumerate all transaction hashes and replay their execution, building a complete reorganized view of contract execution history that was not intended to be publicly accessible?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged user supply a TransactionIdParameter with a future validStart timestamp that wraps around due to convertToNanosMax() overflow, causing the lookup to match a historical transaction with a completely different execution context and returning its opcode trace under the wrong transaction ID?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the getReceiverAddress() fallback logic where ConversionUtils.isLongZero(address) triggers a secondary entity lookup, causing the replay to use a different receiver address than the original transaction used, producing an opcode trace that shows a different execution path and reorganizes the visible transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged user trigger the buildOpcodesResponse() path where recipientAddress equals EMPTY_ADDRESS (Address.ZERO) by querying a CONTRACTCREATEINSTANCE transaction, causing the response to omit the contract address and misrepresent the transaction as having no recipient, reorganizing the visible execution history?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the getValue() method's use of new BigInteger(ethereumTransaction.getValue()) where the raw bytes could represent a different value than the contractResult.getAmount(), causing the replay to execute with a different msg.value than the original transaction and producing a divergent opcode trace?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the getCallDataBytes() fallback from ethereumTransaction.getCallData() to contractResult.getFunctionParameters() when ethereumTransaction is null, causing the replay to use different call data than the original Ethereum transaction used, producing an opcode trace that misrepresents the original execution?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the getEntityAddress() priority logic (evmAddress > alias > entityId) where an entity has both an evmAddress and an alias, causing the replay to use a different address than what was used in the original transaction, reorganizing the visible execution history?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged user submit a transaction hash that maps to a non-contract transaction (e.g., a CRYPTOTRANSFER) in contractTransactionHashRepository, causing contractResultRepository.findById() to throw EntityNotFoundException and leaking that a transaction with that consensus timestamp exists but has no contract result, revealing transaction history structure?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the integer cast '(int) params.getGas() / 3' in OpcodeContext construction where params.getGas() exceeds Integer.MAX_VALUE, causing a negative or zero gas context that makes the replay fail or execute with zero gas, producing a completely different (failed) opcode trace than the original successful transaction?
